mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.8% as of March 2011 reflects post-crisis recovery phase
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand and economic activity, which translates to higher corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 389320 million dollars reflects ongoing consumer spending recovery
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns fail to exceed baseline by at least 2% in periods of RSAFS growth acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) begins to accelerate, creating a negative real rate environment that incentivizes equity allocation over cash, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when the spread widens.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% and CPIAUCSL at 223.49 create negative real rate environment
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with emerging inflation pressures
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative real rate signal, or correlation between real rate spread and forward returns drops below 0.15 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decelerates month-over-month, signaling weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints that compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.5904 reflects post-crisis industrial recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite negative supply signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100k jobs per month while unemployment remains elevated, signaling labor market stalling that reduces consumer income growth and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130738 thousand and UNRATE at 8.8% indicate labor market fragility
regime_hint: weak labor market recovery with stalling job creation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite weak labor signal, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline month-over-month while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.5% annualized, indicating demand-side weakness that signals recession risk and equity market repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month change < -5% AND GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 479 thousand units and GDPC1 at 13380.7 billion reflect post-crisis demand recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite demand weakness signal, or joint effect of HOUST and GDPC1 declines fails to predict negative returns with Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the Federal Reserve maintains accommodative policy (FEDFUNDS < 1%), creating expectations of future rate hikes that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 223.49 and FEDFUNDS at 0.14% create policy lag risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflation-policy lag signal, or the interaction coefficient between inflation and policy rate fails to predict negative returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows above 150k jobs per month, signaling robust supply-side expansion and productivity gains that support earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change > 150000
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.5904 and PAYEMS at 130738 thousand reflect post-crisis recovery momentum
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with strong labor market growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances despite strong supply-labor signal, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window when both conditions are met.
